title: One Child, Two Children
description: Regular plurals (add -s / -es) and the handful of irregulars that never stop showing up.
summary_pt_br: Plurais regulares (apenas -s / -es). Plurais irregulares essenciais: childâ†’children, manâ†’men, womanâ†’women, personâ†’people, footâ†’feet, toothâ†’teeth, mouseâ†’mice. Decorar Ă© o Ăşnico caminho.

scenes.text_en:
Most plurals are easy: one CAT, two CATS. One BOX, two BOXES.
But some words break the rules.
One CHILD â†’ two CHILDREN. One MAN â†’ two MEN. One FOOT â†’ two FEET.
One TOOTH â†’ two TEETH. One PERSON â†’ five PEOPLE. One MOUSE â†’ three MICE.
scenes.text_pt:
A maioria Ă© fĂˇcil: one cat â†’ two cats. One box â†’ two boxes.
Mas alguns fogem da regra.
CrianĂ§a â†’ crianĂ§as (children). Homem â†’ homens (men). PĂ© â†’ pĂ©s (feet).
Dente â†’ dentes (teeth). Pessoa â†’ pessoas (people). Rato â†’ ratos (mice).

scenes.title: Regular plural patterns
scenes.body_en: Just add -S: cat â†’ cats. -CH/-SH/-S/-X/-O often add -ES: box â†’ boxes, watch â†’ watches, tomato â†’ tomatoes. -Y after consonant â†’ -IES: baby â†’ babies, city â†’ cities. Words ending -F/-FE often â†’ -VES: knife â†’ knives, wolf â†’ wolves.
scenes.body_pt: Regras: +S (catâ†’cats). +ES depois de -ch/-sh/-s/-x/-o (boxâ†’boxes). Y depois de consoante â†’ IES (babyâ†’babies). F/FE â†’ VES (knifeâ†’knives).

examples.en: dog â†’ dogs, bus â†’ buses, baby â†’ babies, knife â†’ knives

exercise.question: Plural of 'person'?
exercise.explanation: Plural of PERSON = PEOPLE. 'Persons' is rare and formal.
Five essential irregulars. Drill them daily.

scenes.title: At the fruit market
scenes.pt_summary: Plurais regulares e irregulares â€” fruit/fruits, child/children, foot/feet.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Hello. Would you like some fresh fruits?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes. I want three apples and two oranges.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Of course. Do you need any vegetables?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, four tomatoes and six potatoes.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Are the apples for your children?
turns.pt: As maĂ§ĂŁs sĂŁo para seus filhos?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, I need two loaves of bread.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Here you go. That is twenty dollars.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Thank you. Have a nice day.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "One Child, Two Children". Regular plurals (add -s / -es) and the handful of irregulars that never stop showing up. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key â€” listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice â€” listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave Ă© a prĂˇtica: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus prĂłprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "One Child, Two Children". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'One Child, Two Children'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o tĂ­tulo: "One Child, Two Children".
